key_themes: Mixed analyst ratings with some caution
summary: Viasat has shown robust positive momentum with Q3 2026 earnings significantly beating expectations (EPS $0.79 vs. $0.10) and revenue near targets, alongside the operational rollout of its ViaSat-3 satellite fleet and upgrades from firms like Barclays and Needham. The stock has surged over 600% from lows, hitting new 52-week highs around $64, fueled by defense contracts and spectrum value recognition. While some analysts maintain hold or reduce ratings citing modest growth guidance, the biggest risk is potential delays in satellite commercialization impacting near-term revenue.
